name: Neutron electric dipole moment below PSI limit
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict a neutron electric dipole moment |d_n| below the PSI 2020 upper limit. A nonzero d_n at observable levels would simultaneously signal CP violation beyond the SM and unambiguous time-reversal violation in the strong sector. The current bound directly tightens constraints on the QCD theta angle to |theta_QCD| < ~10^-10.

regime.description: Neutron-scale physics; sensitive to CP-violating new physics up to multi-TeV mass scales depending on couplings.

current_bound.reference: Abel et al. (nEDM), 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020), arXiv:2001.11966

what_it_excludes: Severely constrains supersymmetric extensions with TeV-scale superpartners and natural CP phases (the 'SUSY CP problem'). Forbids many composite Higgs models with large CP-violating couplings. Together with the QCD theta term, motivates the existence of a Peccei-Quinn symmetry and an axion as the natural dynamical solution.

parameterization.name: Effective theta angle (theta_QCD) plus SMEFT CP-violating Wilson coefficients (Weinberg three-gluon operator C_GtildeG, quark chromo-EDMs C_qG, quark EDMs C_eF).
parameterization.reference: Pospelov and Ritz, Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005), arXiv:hep-ph/0504231; Engel, Ramsey-Musolf, van Kolck, PPNP 71, 21 (2013), arXiv:1303.2371.
parameterization.summary: d_n receives contributions from (a) the QCD theta term, with d_n_theta ~ e * theta_QCD * (m_q / Lambda_QCD), and (b) BSM CP phases entering the quark EDMs and chromo-EDMs at low energy via SMEFT operators C_eF^q and C_qG^q.

procedural.predicted_observable: d_n = a_theta * theta_QCD + sum_q (a_q * C_eF^q + b_q * C_qG^q) + a_GG * C_GtildeG + ..., where the a_i, b_i are nucleon matrix elements computed by lattice QCD or chiral perturbation theory.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: theta_QCD < ~10^-10 (the strong CP problem). For BSM phases: |C_eF / Lambda^2|, |C_qG / Lambda^2| <~ (few TeV)^-2 with O(1) phases.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts CP violation beyond the SM, compute its contribution to the QCD theta term and to the relevant SMEFT CP-violating Wilson coefficients.
Use lattice or ChPT matrix elements to convert these into a prediction for d_n. Compare against |d_n| < 1.8 x 10^-26 e cm.
procedural.notes: Lattice-QCD evaluations of nucleon EDM matrix elements (theta term and quark chromo-EDMs) are improving but still have ~30-50% uncertainties. The theta contribution is less precisely calibrated than the quark-EDM contribution.

primary_references.citation: Abel et al. (nEDM Collaboration), 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020)
Pospelov and Ritz, 'Electric dipole moments as probes of new physics', Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005)
